problem: What material stores hydrogen fuel at high density without safety risk?
mechanism: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure)
hypothesis: What material stores hydrogen fuel at high density without safety risk? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Two-dimensional. Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure) directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Mechanical testing (tensile, fatigue) with 14 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: What we observe is not nature itself but nature exposed to our method of questioning. — Heisenberg
